schema.branch: git branch, if a dedicated branch/worktree is in use; null if working directly on main per this repo's concurrent-session convention

conventions: This repo's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md establish direct pushes to main + rebase-before-push as the normal, expected workflow here (AGENTS.md line ~54: 'Direct pushes to main are in use ... git fetch origin main && git rebase origin/main'). RELAY DECISION (2026-07-21, superseding the mission template's mission/admin-* branch suggestion): mission workers commit directly to main on their own cadence, rebasing on origin/main before every push. No dedicated per-worker git worktrees/branches are being created for this mission — that would fight the repo's established single-checkout convention and the separate concurrent 'seven-day evolution' mission that also fast-forwards into this same checkout from C:\Users\jorda\Documents\Codex\worktrees\phantomforce-live-social-analytics-20260712. Claims below are advisory scoping to avoid two agents editing the same files at once in the shared checkout; they are not a substitute for reading git status/log before editing, and are not a hard lock.

Relay (w5) owns: root package.json, package-lock.json (only when a dependency change is unavoidable), tsconfig.base.json, server/prisma/migrations registry + schema.prisma structural/registry-level changes (additive model/field changes inside owned domains stay with the vertical-slice owner), central navigation registration in app/index.html's nav shell, server/src/index.ts route registration/middleware wiring, app/js/main.js, app/phantom.css / app/phantom-skin.css, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, ops/**, scripts/ship-live-admin.mjs, and this .termina/ directory. Relay reviews (does not exclusively hold) security-sensitive changes anywhere, including server/src/access/**, but does not block Forge Systems' primary ownership of that module's implementation.

Forge Systems (w3) owns server/src/access/**, server/src/approval/**, server/src/crm/**, server/src/customization/**, server/src/proposals/**, server/src/sites/**, server/src/workspace-approvals/**, and additive server/prisma/schema.prisma changes within those domains (per Atlas's audit, merged in by Relay).

Forge Experience (w2) owns app/js/crmpipeline.js, app/js/crmprospects.js, app/js/customization.js, app/js/approvalpipeline.js, app/js/proposalpipeline.js, app/js/sitestudio.js, app/js/workspaces.js, app/js/clientsetup.js (per Atlas's audit, merged in by Relay). Explicitly excludes app/js/phantomplay*.js — see pre-existing WIP note below.
Beacon (w4) owns scripts/test-*.mjs and server/scripts/test-*.ts (new/updated), and .termina/artifacts/**.

UNOWNED, flagged by Atlas for Relay to split before assignment: server/src/phantom-ai/** (50+ files — agent-runs/execution, media-lab, brain/neural-spine, security/guard, phantomplay/phantomstore) — too large for one owner, do not assign wholesale. Also unassigned: server/src/managed-growth/**, server/src/client-setup/**, server/src/connectors/**, server/src/falcon/**. Relay will sub-slice these once Atlas's completion matrix lands and their P0/P1 rows are known.

PRE-EXISTING WIP, not claimed by any mission worker, do not touch/stash/revert: app/js/phantomplay.js, server/src/phantom-ai/phantomplay.ts, app/assets/phantomplay/*-cover.svg, app/games/type-storm.html — belongs to a session outside this mission's roster. Leave for its owner to commit.
Vertical-slice owners claim/refine specific paths here before editing when finer granularity than the above is needed; Relay reviews and releases claims at each integration checkpoint.

claims.task: Baseline snapshot, rollback tag, mission-state scaffolding, root-scope survey (read-only)
